rulings.Case Title: MR WIRE COMMUNICATIONS DBA MR WIRE VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ranger Pipelines, Inc., A California Corporation Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents; Compel Production Of Documents; And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday May 18, 2015, Line 5, Defendant Ranger Pipelines, Inc.'s, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents; Compel Production Of Documents; And Request For Monetary Sanctions (part 3 of 3). 3. The Motion to Compel Further Production of Documents: RANGER's motion does not clearly, separately, set forth which RPD's require further production of documents, other than on page 13 of its Points & Authorities. To the extent its Separate Statement regarding Further Written Responses to RPD sometimes also mention seeking further production of documents, such occasional references buried in the Separate Statement are no substitute for a clear statement of the requested relief, set forth in the moving party's brief and/or Notice. As noted above, MR WIRE is required to diligently search and determine whether documents exist in it or its agents possession that are responsive to RANGER's RPD's, and to advise of the results of that diligent search, and whether such responsive documents will be produced, and, if not, why they will not be produced. Having failed to do so, it is difficult for the Court to ascertain if MR WIRE has produced all non-privileged documents responsive to RANGER's RPD's. The Motion to Compel Further Document Production is GRANTED as to RPD's Nos. 1-14, 33, 35, 37, and 38, as specified in RANGER's Points & Authorities, page 13, without prejudice to any further such motion relating to MR WIRE's Further Written Responses to RPD's ordered herein. 4. Request for Award of Sanctions: The court finds that MR WIRE's opposition was not substantially justified in some, but not all, respects. Accordingly, RANGER's request for monetary sanctions is granted against MR WIRE and its counsel in the amount of $2,500.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (see part 1 and 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MR WIRE COMMUNICATIONS DBA MR WIRE VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ranger Pipelines, Inc., A California Corporation Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents; Compel Production Of Documents; And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday May 18, 2015, Line 5, Defendant Ranger Pipelines, Inc.'s, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents; Compel Production Of Documents; And Request For Monetary Sanctions (part 2 of 3). 2. Motion to Compel Further Written Responses to Requests for Documents ("RPD"): The motion is GRANTED as to RPD's 1-20: to the extent that non-privileged responsive documents exist as to each of these RPD's, the Code requires that MR WIRE aver that it has in fact conducted a diligent search, and whether such non-privileged responsive documents exist or not, and whether they will in fact (or have already in fact) been produced; statements of future intent to locate and produce responsive, non-privileged, documents are not Code compliant. A further, verified, written substantive response shall be provided within two weeks from notice of this Order. A further privilege log as to any responsive documents withheld under claim of privilege shall also be provided within two weeks from notice of this Order if such withheld documents have not previously been identified in MR WIRE's initial privilege log. The motion is GRANTED as to RPD's 30-32: The Code requires that MR WIRE aver that it has in fact conducted a diligent search, and whether such non-privileged responsive documents exist or not, and whether they will or will not be produced and the reason why any documents will be withheld. A further, verified, written substantive response shall be provided within two weeks from notice of this Order. A further privilege log as to any responsive documents withheld under claim of privilege shall also be provided within two weeks from notice of this Order if such withheld documents have not previously been identified in MR WIRE's initial privilege log. The motion is GRANTED as to RPD's 35 and 38: given the subject matter of the suit, the RPD's are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and are not overbroad; MR WIRE may not unilaterally circumscribe the scope of the RPD's. The Code requires that MR WIRE aver that it has conducted a diligent search for responsive documents, and whether non-privileged responsive documents exist or not, and whether they will or will not be produced, and the reason why any documents will be withheld. Statements of future intent to locate and produce responsive, non-privileged, documents are not Code compliant. A further, verified, written substantive response shall be provided within two weeks from notice of this Order. A further privilege log as to any responsive documents withheld under claim of privilege shall also be provided within two weeks from notice of this Order if such withheld documents have not previously been identified in MR WIRE's initial privilege log. The motion is GRANTED as to RPD's 39, 40, and 42: The Code requires that MR WIRE aver that it has conducted a diligent search for responsive documents, and whether non-privileged responsive documents exist or not, and whether they will or will not be produced, and the reason why any documents will be withheld. Statements of future intent to locate and produce responsive, non-privileged, documents are not Code compliant. A further, verified, written substantive response shall be provided within two weeks from notice of this Order. A further privilege log as to any responsive documents withheld under claim of privilege shall also be provided within two weeks from notice of this Order if such withheld documents have not previously been identified in MR WIRE's initial privilege log. (see part 1 and 3) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MR WIRE COMMUNICATIONS DBA MR WIRE VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents; Compel Production Of Documents; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday May 18, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT RANGER PIPELINES, INC.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents; Compel Production Of Documents; And Request For Monetary Sanctions (part 1 of 3). Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part, as follows: 1. Motion to Compel Further Answers to Form Interrogatories: The motion is DENIED as to Form Interrogatory nos. 9.1, 9.2, 12.1, 12.2, 12.6, 12.7, 14.1: under the circumstances, the objections are not clearly improper, and MR WIRE has provided substantive responses notwithstanding its objections, and avers those responses are complete. The motion is GRANTED as to No. 12.3: MR WIRE did not object that the response called for privileged material; a further written response accompanied by a privilege log as to as to any such written or recorded statements is required within two weeks from the date of notice of this Order. The motion is DENIED as to Form Interrogatory No. 16.1: the series 16 interrogatories refer to personal injury. The motion is GRANTED as to Form Interrogatories Nos. 50.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5, 50.6: the objection that the interrogatories call for legal conclusions is improper; an amended written response without that objection shall be served within two weeks from the date of notice of this Order. (see part 2 and 3) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order On Further Discovery And Sanctions For Failure To Obey Court Order [Discovery]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday May 18, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOSE MURILLO'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order On Further Discovery And Sanctions For Failure To Obey Court Order [Discovery]. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is DENIED without prejudice to renewal. The Motion as presented is procedurally defective, in that less than the required notice was given for a regularly-scheduled motion served by mail (as indicated by the moving party's Proof of Service). And Defendant Lehigh Consumer Products LLC. Denied without prejudice. Pleadings do not provide adequate explanation how DEHP on clotheslines and described products causes dangers to humans and does not describe alleged means of transmittal. Pleadings consist largely of boilerplate text used in other cases and should be a basis for fee reduction. Exhibits provide little or no assistance to the Court and are almost entirely unrelated to subject matter of the instant case. This should be a basis for fee reduction. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday May 18, 2015, Line 2, Defendant Zinio, Llc'S Omnibus Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery; To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; To Compel Production Of All Responsive Documents; To Compel Attendance At Depositions; To Bar Evidence Of Claimed Account; And Request For Issue, Evidentiary, And Or Terminating Sanctions (part 2 of 2). 5. Plaintiff is ordered to provide substantive written responses to Zinio's Bill of Particulars, without objections, within ten days from notice of this Order and if Plaintiff fails to timely do so then plaintiff shall be deemed precluded from giving evidence in support of the account on which the plaintiff's second cause of action is based, pursuant to C.C.P. §454; 6. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $3,228.00 is awarded to Zinio against plaintiff for plaintiff's failures to respond and abuse of the discovery process; 7. Zinio's request to set a hearing on an Order To Show Cause to strike plaintiff's Complaint and/or answer to Zinio's Cross-Complaint is DENIED. Zinio shall prepare a formal Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: UBIKA SOLUTIONS, INC VS. ZINIO, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Omnibus Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery; To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; To Compel Production Of All Responsive Documents; To Compel Attendance At Depositions; To Bar Evidence Of Claimed Account; And Request For Issue, Evidentiary, And Or Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday May 18, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT ZINIO, LLC'S Omnibus Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery; To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; To Compel Production Of All Responsive Documents; To Compel Attendance At Depositions; To Bar Evidence Of Claimed Account; And Request For Issue, Evidentiary, And Or Terminating Sanctions (part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No Opposition was received to the pending omnibus motion. The omnibus motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part, as follows: 1. Plaintiff has failed to timely respond to the written discovery that are the subject of this motion. Plaintiff is deemed to have waived all objections to the written discovery and plaintiff is ordered to provide substantive written responses, without any objections, to moving party Zinio's Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Requests for Documents, within ten days from notice of this Order; 2. Plaintiff is ordered to produce any and all responsive documents, without withholding any responsive documents based upon any objection whatsoever, within ten days from notice of this Order; 3. Zinio's Requests for Admissions are hereby deemed admitted; 4. Verexo, Inc. is ordered to produce the Person Most Qualified to testify on its behalf as to the matters listed in Zinio's Notice of Deposition dated January 29, 2015 (attached as Exhibit H to the Declaration of James Smith in Support of the Motion) on a business day to be unilaterally designated by Zinio for a date no sooner than three weeks after the date of notice of this Order; (see part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELO "TONY" PATRUNO VS. JIMMY F. FOUST ET AL*** TRANSFERRED TO SAN JOAQUIN COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT***
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendants Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 18, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANTS JIMMY FOUST, DESIREE AMOR, SHILOH FOUST, ALMA MATER, INC., AND VOL-TEN CORPORATION, INC.'S Motion To Transfer Venue. The motion to change venue is granted. The case is transferred to San Joaquin County where all of the defendants reside. Plaintiff cannot establish San Francisco venue based on the contract claim. Only Mr. Faust is a party to that contract and the co-defendants are not. The alter ego allegations do not change the analysis because the co-defendants cannot be the alter ego of an individual (Mr. Foust). The personal injury exception does not apply. Defendants correctly cite Carruth v. Sup. Crt. (1978) 80 Cal.App.3d 215, 219-220. This case does not allege physical injury or physical property damage. The request for fees is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FRANKLIN HAYES BUILDERS CORP. VS. LINDEN HAYES OWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Stay Pending Arbitration (Control Date)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 18, 2015, Line 8, Plaintiff Franklin Hayes Builders Corp.'s Motion To Stay Pending Arbitration. Grant, no opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
